title: Children
wikitext:
"'''Children'''" is a term used to refer to the 14-year-old pilots of the [[Evangelions]]. The original Japanese dub of the series used "children" even for a single pilot, hence [[Shinji Ikari]] is the "Third Children". The original English dub by ADV films changed this to "Child" (i.e. "Third Child"), as they felt it sounded unnatural in English.

The children are supposedly chosen by the [[Marduk Institute]], but in reality they are chosen by [[Nerv]] as the Institute is a fictitious entity.

The Children are:

*[[Rei Ayanami]] - First Children
*[[Asuka Langley Soryu]] - Second Children
*[[Shinji Ikari]] - Third Children
*[[Toji Suzuhara]] - Fourth Children
*[[Kaworu Nagisa]] - Fifth Children

The numbering system is based on the order in which the Eva pilots were selected. Asuka enjoys taunting Shinji by referring to him as the "Third Children", pointing out that she is the "Second Children", as if being selected before Shinji quantifiably makes her superior to him.

==Rebuild of Evangelion==
In the [[Rebuild of Evangelion]] series, the expression "(n)th Children" is not used. Instead, the pilots are referred to in terms such as Ichibanme no tekikakusha (一番目の適格者, "The First Qualified Person/Candidate"), Dai Ichi no Shōjo (第一の少女, "First Girl"), Sanninme no kodomo (三人目の子供, "Third Child"), or Dai San no Shōnen (第三の少年, "Third Boy").

==Notes==
[[Image:Children kodomotachi.jpg|right|thumb|250px|"And to all the Children..."]]

*According to [https://web.archive.org/web/20111002185656/http://eva.onegeek.org/pipermail/oldeva/1999-November/032029.html a FAQ from the Eva Mailing List], the term "Children" is used for the pilots because Rei Ayanami was not one child but the first of many identical clones, and therefore is a series of children. It is implied that [[Nerv]] used the ungrammatical term "Children" for all the other pilots to hide the fact that Rei has clones.

*The term "Children" is also used for the final [[wikt:telop|telop]] in [[Episode 26]] ("And to all the Children, congratulations!"), giving the term a more universal feel instead of just referring to the Eva pilots. The furigana チルドレン (''children'') is used here instead of the kanji 子供達 (''kodomo tachi'').

[[Category:A to Z]]
[[Category:Terminology]]
html:
"Children" is a term used to refer to the 14-year-old pilots of the Evangelions. The original Japanese dub of the series used "children" even for a single pilot, hence Shinji Ikari is the "Third Children". The original English dub by ADV films changed this to "Child" (i.e. "Third Child"), as they felt it sounded unnatural in English.

The children are supposedly chosen by the Marduk Institute, but in reality they are chosen by Nerv as the Institute is a fictitious entity.

The Children are:

  • Rei Ayanami - First Children
  • Asuka Langley Soryu - Second Children
  • Shinji Ikari - Third Children
  • Toji Suzuhara - Fourth Children
  • Kaworu Nagisa - Fifth Children

The numbering system is based on the order in which the Eva pilots were selected. Asuka enjoys taunting Shinji by referring to him as the "Third Children", pointing out that she is the "Second Children", as if being selected before Shinji quantifiably makes her superior to him.

Rebuild of Evangelion[edit]

In the Rebuild of Evangelion series, the expression "(n)th Children" is not used. Instead, the pilots are referred to in terms such as Ichibanme no tekikakusha (一番目の適格者, "The First Qualified Person/Candidate"), Dai Ichi no Shōjo (第一の少女, "First Girl"), Sanninme no kodomo (三人目の子供, "Third Child"), or Dai San no Shōnen (第三の少年, "Third Boy").

Notes[edit]

  • According to a FAQ from the Eva Mailing List, the term "Children" is used for the pilots because Rei Ayanami was not one child but the first of many identical clones, and therefore is a series of children. It is implied that Nerv used the ungrammatical term "Children" for all the other pilots to hide the fact that Rei has clones.
  • The term "Children" is also used for the final telop in Episode 26 ("And to all the Children, congratulations!"), giving the term a more universal feel instead of just referring to the Eva pilots. The furigana チルドレン (children) is used here instead of the kanji 子供達 (kodomo tachi).
